source_jobs.title: Senior Full Stack Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Anthuria is bringing AI innovation to skilled nursing facilities, transforming how nurses and operators manage critical healthcare information. We use AI agents to turn overwhelming volumes of clinical notes into clear, actionable insights that immediately improve patient care, reimbursement and compliance. Founded only a few months ago and backed by a family office with deep healthcare expertise, we are already deployed in 180 facilities across 10+ states with paying customers.

We're building with experts from the industry who have joined the team. Our understanding of our users’ operations enables us to create products that become essential to our users' daily workflows in an industry where technological innovation has historically lagged.

At Anthuria, we foster a culture of collaboration, support, and curiosity. We assume best intentions, pursue technical excellence, and remain focused on delivering client value. As an early team member, you'll continuously shape not just our technical direction but our culture and practices as well. This is a rare opportunity to make a massive impact from the ground floor as we build something transformative in healthcare.

 

You Will

Architect and build full-stack solutions: You will work across our entire technology stack to deliver high quality features using Python, React, and AWS. You have experience building complex web applications with robust CI/CD pipelines and cloud monitoring systems, and have led projects from concept to scale across frontend, backend, and infrastructure layers.

Craft high-leverage, intuitive products: You will design and ship products that feel simple and seamless to use, even when the underlying workflows are complex. You focus on reducing cognitive load, eliminating unnecessary steps, and enabling nurses and operators to complete critical tasks quickly and confidently within the realities of skilled nursing operations.

Navigate healthcare systems and data requirements: You'll design and implement solutions that effectively integrate with Electronic Health Records (EHRs) while maintaining compliance with healthcare regulations like HIPAA.

Collaborate across organizational boundaries: You will work with internal teams and directly with clients (nurses and operators) to translate domain-specific challenges into technical solutions. You can effectively communicate technical concepts to diverse stakeholders.

Uphold engineering best practices: As an early team member, you will help set technical standards and build scalable foundations that will support our growth. You have experience establishing engineering processes and architectural patterns that balance speed needs with long-term maintainability.

 

You Are

Highly accountable: You take ownership of your work from concept to maintenance, treating the company's success as your own. You approach challenges with an owner's mindset, taking responsibility for outcomes and maintaining the highest standards of integrity in all you do.

Curious and collaborative: You continuously learn while generously offering help without being asked. You bring a growth mindset to everything you do, passionate about developing your own capabilities while lifting the entire organization in the process.

Comfortable with ambiguity: You navigate uncertainty with confidence and excitement, seeing the opportunity to shape a growing company's foundation. You are independent and self-directed, thriving in an early-stage environment where your impact is immediately visible.
Value-driven: You ship quickly without sacrificing necessary quality. You pragmatically balance speed of iteration with high technical standards, always keeping business impact as your north star when making decisions.

 

What We Offer


PAY TRANSPARENCY

The annual base salary range for this position is $190,000 - $240,000. Actual compensation offered to the successful candidate may vary from the posted hiring range based upon work experience, and/or skill le

source_jobs.title: Staff Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
At TriEdge Investments, we build technology that helps real businesses operate better. Working across a portfolio of 30+ companies and select partners, we embed with operators to design and ship automation that moves the numbers, including throughput, cost, and reliability. Over time, we turn those hard-won solutions into platform tools that scale across companies, building an intelligent system that brings modern automation to businesses typically overlooked by traditional software.

As a Staff Software Engineer, you will operate as a senior technical leader embedded with operators and clients, owning complex problem spaces from discovery through production. Rather than executing against predefined specs, you will identify the highest-leverage opportunities, shape requirements in partnership with users and executives, and define system architectures that can be scaled and reused across the portfolio. You will remain hands-on in code while setting technical direction, ensuring solutions are durable, secure, and aligned with long-term business outcomes.

What You’ll Do

 - Lead deep technical discovery with operators and clients, diagnosing workflows, systemic pain points, and high-leverage automation opportunities across engagements

 - Own architecture and delivery of end-to-end systems spanning web, cloud, data, and AI infrastructure, with an eye toward reuse and long-term scalability

 - Define and build robust APIs, agentic workflows, and AI/ML-driven systems, setting standards for how intelligent automation is designed and deployed

 - Rapidly prototype, develop, validate, and harden solutions in live client environments, balancing speed with production-grade reliability and adoption

 - Set technical direction for performance, scalability, security, and resilience, proactively identifying risks and addressing them before they impact customers

 - Lead cross-functional technical alignment with product, data, and operators to ensure solutions map directly to business outcomes and portfolio strategy

 - Own production excellence in client deployments, driving root-cause analysis, durable fixes, and systemic improvements rather than one-off patches

 - Serve as a senior technical authority and trusted advisor to executives and operators while remaining deeply hands-on in code and system design

About You

 - 7+ years of full-stack software engineering experience, with demonstrated Staff-level impact in production environments

 - Proven experience in leveraging AI development stack to develop enterprise grade applications from end to end, such as Claude code, custom mcp, skills, backend-as-a-service.

 - Proven track record of owning ambiguous, high-stakes problem spaces end to end, including direct delivery in client- or operator-facing contexts

 - Broad technical depth across the stack (e.g., Python, React/Next.js, SQL/NoSQL, cloud infrastructure), with the judgment to choose appropriate tools and architectures

 - Strong API and systems design expertise, including defining interfaces and patterns that scale beyond a single team or deployment

 - Hands-on experience integrating AI/ML into production systems, with an understanding of tradeoffs across performance, cost, reliability, and compliance

 - Exceptional communicator, capable of influencing engineers, operators, and executives through clear technical reasoning and written design artifacts

 - Pragmatic technical leader with a strong bias toward ownership, accountability, and forward-deployed execution

 - Effective operating in complex environments, including legacy systems, heterogeneous stacks, and regulated or compliance-driven domains

 - Fluent in AI-assisted development, using modern tooling to increase leverage while maintaining high standards for code quality and system integrity

 - Bachelor’s degree minimum

Why Join TriEdge Investments

 - Play a key role in reshaping private equity with AI-powered platforms that scale

 - Help digitize and transform rea

source_jobs.title: Co-Head, Axiom Private Credit
source_jobs.description:
Co-Head, Axiom Private Credit



About Axiom Private Credit

Axiom Private Credit is a new, healthcare lending platform established under TriEdge Investments as a standalone strategy. The platform provides senior and subordinated mezzanine, secured financing to transitional skilled nursing and senior-care assets, including skilled nursing, assisted living, memory care, and independent living facilities. Rather than operating as a conventional balance-sheet lender, Axiom serves as an active bridge lender offering creative solutions including participating in the upside through preferred returns or warrants. Built on Hypha, TriEdge’s proprietary AI platform, Axiom is positioned to deploy efficiently and to expand beyond skilled nursing into healthcare credit more broadly.



About TriEdge Investments

TriEdge is a family office focused on building enduring value across healthcare, financial services, cybersecurity, defense technology and beyond. Founded in 2024 by Simcha Hyman, who brings 15+ years of experience building and exiting companies in healthcare and finance, TriEdge takes a multi-strategy approach, investing across stages and verticals including private equity, venture capital, real estate, and company incubation. With offices in New York City, Miami, and Tel Aviv, TriEdge brings together a team of seasoned operators, technologists, and investors to partner with founders building the technology-enabled platforms of tomorrow. Equally important to the mission is impact investing and philanthropy with a focus on mental health, education, and combating poverty and antisemitism. To learn more about our work, visit: https://www.triedgeinvestments.com/



What We’re Really Looking For

We are seeking an experienced and analytically rigorous individual to launch and lead Axiom Credit as its full-time operating principal, working alongside a part-time co-head who provides legal and deal-execution oversight. The successful candidate will develop and oversee underwriting, origination and credit review; manage the platform’s assets on Hypha; and support expansion into adjacent healthcare verticals over time.



Key Responsibilities

· Build and manage a team to own end-to-end underwriting for transitional skilled nursing and senior-care credits, structuring each transaction with appropriate use of preferred returns, warrants, and downside protection.

· Lead origination across the platform’s channels, including direct borrower relationships, brokers, and opportunities sourced through banking relationships.

· Actively manage the loan portfolio, monitoring credit performance and intervening early to protect returns.

· Develop the platform’s technology (Hypha) across underwriting, credit policy, covenant monitoring, servicing, and compliance, translating transaction logic into structured workflows.

· Manage the pace and concentration of deployment across a high volume of small-balance transactions (generally under $20 million).

· Support the platform’s growth, including relationships with lenders and prospective institutional partners, as the strategy scales toward larger facilities and additional verticals.



Who You Are

· 3–7 years of experience in credit, special situations, distressed, or structured lending, ideally from a leading credit or investment platform; candidates above this range will be considered for the right profile.

· An advanced and creative underwriter, capable of structuring non-standard, transitional credit and underwriting to a managed downside.

· Proactive in generating origination and deal flow, in addition to underwriting inbound opportunities.

· Able to pair rigorous credit analysis with the adaptability to build new processes in a fast-evolving environment.

· Motivated by developing underwriting and asset-management workflows on an AI platform (Hypha).

· Entrepreneurial and effective in a fast-moving environment.

· Healthcare, skilled nursing, or private-credit experience is an advantage rathe

source_jobs.title: People Associate
source_jobs.description:
People Associate

About TriEdge Investments

TriEdge Investments is a family office and technology company based in New York. We make concentrated investments in a few companies. We also incubate technology companies to solve the problems our investment team observes in our portfolio companies. In this sense, we've proven it's a true industry problem. We only focus on select healthcare verticals. Our mission is to improve patient outcomes and reduce the complexity of healthcare through better investing and technology. To do this, we find and foster extraordinary people who can think hyper-rationally yet with creativity. Our talent philosophy is that to do something different, you must be different. We enjoy working with people who think so too.

What We're Really Looking For

We’re looking for a highly organized, detail-oriented People Associate to help deliver an exceptional employee experience across the full employee lifecycle. In this role, you will partner closely with employees, leaders, and external partners to support People Operations, employee programs, talent initiatives, and organizational growth.

You’ll play a key role in ensuring a seamless employee experience, maintaining operational excellence across people processes, supporting managers and employees, and helping scale People practices as the organization grows. This is an opportunity for someone who enjoys both strategic and operational work and wants to contribute meaningfully within a high-performance, fast-growing environment.

Key Responsibilities

 - Drive people initiatives across onboarding, performance management, compensation, employee development, engagement, and other employee lifecycle programs

 - Partner with leadership on people reporting and analytics, supporting workforce planning, headcount reporting, turnover analysis, and other people insights

 - Facilitate employee equity administration, coordinating with legal counsel, employees, and internal stakeholders

 - Partner with external immigration counsel, employees, and hiring managers to facilitate visa and immigration processes

 - Develop and maintain HR resources, presentations, communications, training materials, and employee-facing documentation

 - Identify opportunities to improve employee experience, strengthen people operations, and build scalable processes that support organizational growth

 - Manage employee offboarding processes, including payroll coordination, documentation, systems access, and asset return logistics

 - Partner with employees and leaders throughout the employee lifecycle, helping deliver a consistent, high-quality employee experience, and serve as a trusted resource for employee questions and people-related matters, providing guidance and escalating issues when appropriate

 - Prepare and facilitate offer letter generation, background checks, and pre-employment documentation for new hires

 - Lead onboarding processes, including coordination of welcome communications, technology setup, benefits enrollment, and cross-functional stakeholder engagement to ensure a smooth transition into the organization

 - Partner with our PEO and internal stakeholders on employee record management, benefits administration, payroll processes, compliance matters, and policy execution

 - Contribute to strategic People initiatives, departmental goals, and other projects as needed

Who You Are

 - Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, Psychology, or a related field

 - 4+ years of experience in Human Resources, People Operations, People Business Partnering, or related roles

 - High integrity with the ability to handle sensitive employee information with discretion, professionalism, and sound judgment

 - Strong skills in the Microsoft Office Suite

 - Tech-savvy and able to learn new tools quickly; experience with PEO platforms, HRIS, payroll systems, and productivity tools (e.g., Slack, Asana, etc.) is preferred

 - Experience supporting compensation a

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant (Miami Office)
source_jobs.description:
Executive Assistant



About TriEdge Investments

TriEdge is a family office focused on building enduring value across healthcare, financial services, cybersecurity, defense technology and beyond. Founded in 2024 by Simcha Hyman, who brings 15+ years of experience building and exiting companies in healthcare and finance, TriEdge takes a multi-strategy approach, investing across stages and verticals including private equity, venture capital, real estate, and company incubation. With offices in New York City, Miami, and Tel Aviv, TriEdge brings together a team of seasoned operators, technologists, and investors to partner with founders building the technology-enabled platforms of tomorrow. Equally important to the mission is impact investing and philanthropy with a focus on mental health, education, and combating poverty and antisemitism. To learn more about our work, visit: https://www.triedgeinvestments.com/



What We're Really Looking For

We are looking for a high-judgment Executive Assistant to support our Founder/CEO across company-building, investor and partner engagement, and day-to-day executive execution. This person will serve as an operational force multiplier: protecting the executive’s time, driving follow-through, coordinating high-stakes meetings, events and communications, and helping him stay focused on the relationships, decisions, and opportunities where he adds the most value.



Key Responsibilities

· Manage the CEO’s complex calendar, priorities, and scheduling with strong judgment and proactive decision-making; evaluate effective use of time and serve as a gatekeeper to ensure focus on high value areas.

· Prepare meeting agendas, meeting notes, and follow-up trackers; capture decisions and task owners and ensure next steps are executed.

· Coordinate internal and external meetings across leadership, investors, founders, partners, and advisors.

· Support special projects, board/investor materials coordination, and other ad hoc executive priorities.

· Manage special events, hosting needs, and all logistics to ensure seamless execution and a positive attendee experience.

· Handle personal tasks related to family matters and activities including gift giving, holiday events, research and engagement of services and other ad hoc tasks with discretion and professionalism.

· Build simple systems and processes that improve organization, visibility, and follow-through.

· Serve as a trusted point of coordination for sensitive, confidential matters requiring sound judgment and follow-through.



Who You Are

· 5+ years supporting a CEO, founder or senior executive in a demanding, high-velocity environment.

· Outstanding organizational skills and attention to detail.

· Strong judgment, discretion, and professionalism.

· Ability to manage competing priorities and stay calm under pressure.

· Proactive, resourceful, and comfortable working independently with minimal direction.

· Utilizes emerging technologies for maximum efficiency.

· Knowledgeable about Jewish practices, the community and the resulting calendar, scheduling, travel, traditions and hospitality considerations.



Location

TriEdge is an in-person team with offices in Hudson Yards, New York, Miami, Florida and Tel Aviv, Israel. This role will be based in Miami, Florida, and will be in office Monday - Thursday, working remotely on Fridays. Travel will be required from time to time.



Pay Transparency

The annual base salary range for this position is $130,000-$150,000, which does not include discretionary bonus compensation or our comprehensive benefits package. Actual compensation offered to the successful candidate may vary from posted hiring range based upon work experience, education, and/or skill level, among other things. This role is eligible for an annual performance bonus.





Please note: We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to diversity and inclusion. All qualified applicants will receive considerat

source_jobs.title: Salud, AVP of People Operations
source_jobs.description:
Associate Vice President, People Operations

Job Description:

Salud Healthcare is a tech-forward healthcare services organization that helps private practices transition to alternative payment models. The AVP, People Operations will lead human resources and talent acquisition to support our workforce and drive organizational success.



Job Responsibilities:

· Responsible for advancing the culture at Salud Healthcare through input and development of employee handbooks and policies, fringe benefit offering, and the development of ERGs as appropriate.

· Responsible for the procurement and development of an enterprise ERP solution and ensure the accuracy of the HRIS data and affiliated systems.

· Develop an annual review process for the company, including review templates and calibration guides.

· Responsible for the recruitment of key onshore positions and partnering with vendors and strategic partners on any offshore roles.

· Develop a robust onboarding process for new employees,

· Architect and cultivate a culture of ongoing learning for existing employees, including learning modules for employees promoted into managerial roles.

· Ensure compliance with federal and state employment laws and regulations.

· Providing guidance and support to managers and employees on HR-related matters, experience with US immigration visas is a plus.

· Lead diversity and inclusion efforts to promote a diverse and inclusive workplace.

· Collaborate with cross-functional teams to develop and execute HR projects and initiatives.



Qualifications:

· Bachelor’s degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or a related field.

· Minimum of 5-7 years of progressive HR or talent management experience

· 3+ years’ experience in a managerial capacity

· Proven track record of implementing HR strategies and initiatives.

· Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.

· Demonstrated experience in talent acquisition, performance management, and employee engagement.

· Professional HR certification (e.g., PHR, SPHR) is highly desirable.

· Ability to work in our corporate office 4-5 days a week in Miramar, FL.

· Bi-lingual (English/Spanish) is a plus

· Ability to travel 10-20% locally and out of state from time-to-time



Please note: We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to diversity and inclusion. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, ethnicity, sex, age, national origin, citizenship status, disability, marital status, partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or local law.

source_jobs.title: Clinical Sales Specialist (MDS / Post-Acute)
source_jobs.description:
CLINICAL SALES SPECIALIST (MDS / POST-ACUTE)

Location: NYC (Hybrid)

Our Company

Anthuria is transforming senior living with AI. Our platform leverages large language models to turn dense clinical documents into clear, actionable insights—improving patient care, reimbursement, and workflow efficiency. Built within the TriEdge family office, we are already deployed across 10+ states.

We’re building the first AI operating system designed specifically for skilled nursing and post-acute workflows—uniting teams and data across care, finance, and compliance. At Anthuria, we value candor, curiosity, and client-first execution. This is a rare chance to bring groundbreaking technology to the bedside and directly impact patient care across the country.


The Role

We’re hiring a Clinical Sales Specialist to drive our revenue execution on the front lines. You will own the full sales lifecycle for mid-market and enterprise SNF operators, acting as the critical bridge between our AI technology and the daily realities of post-acute care.

Because our solutions deeply impact clinical documentation and compliance, we are looking for someone with profound, firsthand knowledge of MDS (Minimum Data Set) and post-acute reimbursement. You will leverage this clinical expertise to demonstrate exactly how Anthuria improves acuity capture, reduces workflow friction, and unites care teams.


You Will

 - Own the full sales cycle: Lead deals from initial outbound outreach to signed contract across SNF and assisted living operators.

 - Leverage Clinical Expertise: Connect the dots between Anthuria’s AI capabilities and facility pain points around MDS, PDPM, and clinical compliance to drive undeniable financial and operational ROI.

 - Drive growth channels: Execute outbound campaigns, qualify inbound leads, attend industry conferences, and build local referral partnerships.

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: Partner with Product and Clinical teams to tailor demos, refine messaging, and translate our value proposition into language that resonates with Directors of Nursing, Administrators, and MDS Coordinators.

 - Negotiate and close: Lead pricing, proposal, and contract conversations with empathy, speed, and precision.

 - Inform the roadmap: Surface field learnings, feature requests, and customer workflow insights to guide our product strategy.

 - Champion customer success: Ensure smooth handoffs to the implementation team to support long-term customer value and retention.


You Are

 - Have deep, firsthand knowledge of the SNF/post-acute market, specifically regarding MDS workflows, PDPM, and clinical documentation requirements. (Former MDS Coordinators, RNs, or clinical consultants transitioning into sales are highly encouraged to apply.)

 - Have 2+ years of quota-carrying B2B SaaS/tech sales experience, OR a strong clinical consulting background with a proven aptitude and hunger for direct sales.

 - Know how to navigate healthcare operator structures and speak confidently to both clinical leaders and financial stakeholders.

 - Are a strong communicator who can simplify complex AI and workflow tech for diverse audiences.

 - Are highly organized, self-motivated, and thrive in an agile, early-stage environment where you manage your own pipeline.

 - Care deeply about solving real-world problems that improve care delivery and reduce operational chaos for nurses.


What We Offer

Pay Transparency

The annual base salary range for this position is $120,000 - $150,000. Actual compensation offered to the successful candidate may vary from the posted hiring range based upon work experience and/or skill level, among other things. This role also includes an aggressive, uncapped commission structure.

Benefits

 - $0 deductible and 100% employee-covered health, vision, and dental benefit package

 - 401(k) matching program of 50% up to 6% of annual salary

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Beautiful custom-built office in NY with daily lunch

Location

Anthuria is

source_jobs.title: Head of Sales & GTM
source_jobs.description:
HEAD OF SALES & GTM

Location: NYC (Hybrid)



ABOUT ANTHURIA

Anthuria is transforming senior living with AI. Our platform leverages large language models to turn dense clinical documents into clear, actionable insights—improving patient care, reimbursement, and workflow efficiency. Built within the TriEdge family office, we are already deployed across 10+ states.

We’re building the first AI operating system designed specifically for skilled nursing and post-acute workflows—uniting teams and data across care, finance, and compliance. At Anthuria, we value candor, curiosity, and client-first execution. This is a rare chance to shape a GTM engine from the ground up and directly impact patient care across the country.




THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Head of Sales & GTM to lead our revenue strategy and execution. You’ll own the full sales lifecycle, craft scalable playbooks, and build foundational growth processes across Sales and Marketing. You’ll also scale the go-to-market team, including managing a Customer Success Lead. As a founding team member, you’ll play a key role in shaping our culture and driving commercial success.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Own the full sales cycle: Lead deals from outbound to signed contract across mid-market and enterprise SNF operators

 - Build the GTM plan: Define ICP, craft market and competitive positioning, map buying personas, and create quarterly planning roadmaps

 - Build repeatable GTM motion: Design insight-driven sales processes, manage pipeline and operations in Salesforce, and optimize funnel performance

 - Drive growth channels: Execute outbound campaigns, attend conferences, build referral partnerships, and qualify inbound leads

 - Collaborate cross-functionally: Partner with Product and Clinical to tailor demos, refine messaging, and translate value into clinical and financial impact

 - Negotiate and close: Lead pricing, proposal, and contract conversations with empathy, speed, and precision

 - Revenue forecast and reporting: Deliver growth projections, conversion metrics, and sales insights to executive leadership

 - Inform the roadmap: Surface field learnings and customer pain points to guide product and GTM strategy

 - Champion customer success: Ensure smooth handoffs to implementation and support long-term customer value and retention


YOU MIGHT BE A FIT IF YOU:

 - Have 5+ years of B2B SaaS sales experience with a proven track record of closing multi-stakeholder deals

 - Know how to sell into healthcare operators and speak to both clinical and financial stakeholders

 - Are a strong communicator who can simplify complex tech for diverse audiences

 - Are highly organized, data-driven, and thrive in early-stage environments

 - Care about solving real-world problems that improve care delivery and reduce operational chaos


BONUS POINTS

 - Experience selling healthcare SaaS (EHR, analytics, AI, or care coordination tools)

 - Led GTM or sales initiatives in a startup or zero-to-one environment

 - Built growth strategies to drive top-of-funnel and outbound performance
   
   


WHAT WE OFFER

Pay Transparency

The annual base salary range for this position is $180,000-$220,000. Actual compensation offered to the successful candidate may vary from the posted hiring range based upon work experience, and/or skill level, among other things.

Benefits

 - $0 deductible and 100% employee covered health, vision, and dental benefit package

 - 401(k) matching program of 50% up to 6% of annual salary.

 - Unlimited PTO

 - Beautiful custom-built office in NY with daily lunch

Location

Anthuria is headquartered in a brand new office space in New York's Hudson Yards. We've designed our workplace to foster the collaboration and spontaneous interactions that drive innovation. Our team works in-office four days per week, with flexibility to work remotely when needed.

source_jobs.title: AVP, Managed Care Contracting & Medical Economics
source_jobs.description:
Salud Healthcare

Salud Healthcare is transforming the delivery of value-based care by empowering provider organizations with data-driven insights, modern technology, and operational excellence. As a rapidly growing Management Services Organization (MSO), Salud partners with physician groups to improve patient outcomes, optimize clinical performance, and navigate the complexities of value-based care. Our technology platform serves as the connective tissue between providers, payers, analytics, and operations—helping healthcare organizations close care gaps, improve quality outcomes, and drive better financial performance. We are building the next generation of healthcare applications that power population health management, risk adjustment, care coordination, analytics, and provider engagement.

 

Position Summary

This role will lead our Managed Care Contracting & Medical Economics business unit. This department is responsible for conducting quantitative analysis to determine organizational risks of participating in various value-based care programs. In addition, this person will be responsible for engaging with payor partners on negotiating both FFS and value-based arrangements for our provider network affiliates.



Responsibilities:

 - Apply quantitative analysis to underwrite risk across various value-based care contracts, including but not limited to P4Q, professional fee capitation, two-sided shared savings models, and global capitation arrangements.

 - A strong understanding of healthcare terminology and principles, including medical coding, claims data, and broader medical economic trends, including Part A, B, and D segments.

 - Strong proficiency in being able to use databases and tools to query, interpret, and build sensitivity models to evaluate risk propensity.

 - Ability to take claims data and develop IBNR models to inform a prediction of future paid claims experience.

 - Partner cross-functionally with internal stakeholders on legal implications of managed care negotiations, financial forecasting and budgeting, and operational delivery tactics.

 - Effective verbal, written, and presentation skills to wide audiences, including physicians, executive management, and external stakeholders.

 - Responsible for participating in payor JOC meetings to facilitate alignment on performance targets and goals.



Qualifications:

 - Bachelor’s degree (B.A.) in actuarial science, healthcare economics, quantitative analysis, mathematics, or a closely related field; a statistics background would prove helpful in this role

 - Experienced healthcare economist or actuary with a payor or benefits consultant background.

 - ASA or FSA designation required, or progress towards such designations.

 - Experience in client-facing roles

 - Strong analytical abilities and proficiency with Microsoft Office, especially Word, Excel, and PowerPoint.

 - Experience across different health insurance products is highly preferred, including: Medicare, Medicaid, commercial, and exchange lines of business.
   
   

Location: Miramar, FL (Hybrid or Remote)



Compensation & Benefits

 - $150 - 200K base salary compensation. Actual compensation offered to the successful candidate may vary from the posted hiring range based on work experience, skill level, and other factors.

 - Health, dental, and vision insurance.

 - 401(k)

 - Paid time off and company holidays.

 - Opportunity to shape the future of healthcare technology within a rapidly growing value-based care organization.

Please note: We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer, and we are committed to diversity and inclusion. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, ethnicity, sex, age, national origin, citizenship status, disability, marital status, partnership status, sexual orientation, gender identity and expression, military or veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state or loca

source_jobs.title: Principal Software Engineer, Healthcare Platform
source_jobs.description:
Salud Healthcare

Salud Healthcare is transforming how value-based care is delivered by empowering provider organizations with data-driven insights, modern technology, and operational excellence. As a rapidly growing Management Services Organization (MSO), Salud partners with physician groups to improve patient outcomes, optimize clinical performance, and navigate the complexities of value-based care. Our technology platform serves as the connective tissue between providers, payers, analytics, and operations—helping healthcare organizations close care gaps, improve quality outcomes, and drive better financial performance. We are building the next generation of healthcare applications that power population health management, risk adjustment, care coordination, analytics, and provider engagement.



Position Summary

Salud Healthcare is seeking a Principal Software Engineer to help architect, build, and scale the technology foundation that powers our value-based care platform.

This is a hands-on, player-coach role for an experienced engineer who enjoys balancing software development, technical leadership, architecture, and mentorship. You will serve as a key technical partner to the VP of Technology & Data Analytics, helping transform healthcare business requirements into scalable software solutions that support providers, payers, and operational teams.

You will lead development across a portfolio of healthcare applications spanning population health analytics, care management, risk adjustment, provider engagement, and operational workflows. While this role includes leadership responsibilities, we are specifically looking for someone who remains close to the code, enjoys solving complex technical problems, and can lead through technical credibility rather than organizational hierarchy.

The ideal candidate brings a strong combination of healthcare technology experience, software architecture expertise, and practical engineering leadership. They understand how healthcare data moves through systems, can navigate provider and payer workflows, and possess the judgment to build durable solutions that balance immediate business needs with long-term platform scalability.

Success in this role will be measured by your ability to establish scalable technical foundations, deliver production-ready applications, improve engineering practices, reduce technical debt, and serve as a trusted technical advisor across technology, analytics, operations, and executive leadership.



Key Responsibilities

Technical Architecture & Platform Leadership

 - Partner with the VP of Technology & Data Analytics to influence architecture decisions, platform strategy, and technical direction.

 - Lead architectural design reviews across Salud's growing application portfolio.

 - Design scalable systems supporting healthcare workflows, analytics platforms, and operational applications.

 - Establish engineering standards around APIs, authentication, observability, reliability, and maintainability.

 - Evaluate technical tradeoffs and provide recommendations balancing speed, scalability, and long-term sustainability.

 - Serve as a trusted technical advisor across engineering, analytics, operations, and executive leadership.

Software Engineering & Product Development

 - Remain actively involved in software development and contribute production-quality code.

 - Lead engineering efforts across internal and external-facing healthcare applications.

 - Design and build backend services, APIs, orchestration layers, workflow automation systems, and healthcare data integrations.

 - Collaborate closely with analytics, operations, and product stakeholders to translate healthcare business requirements into scalable software solutions.

 - Ensure all applications meet security, compliance, and performance requirements.

Healthcare Data & Interoperability

 - Build and maintain integrations across EMRs, claims systems, payer data, and operational healthcare platforms

source_jobs.title: Senior AI Applied Scientist
source_jobs.description:
Senior AI Applied Scientist

Our Company

Anthuria is bringing AI innovation to skilled nursing facilities, transforming how nurses and operators manage critical healthcare information. We use AI agents to turn overwhelming volumes of clinical notes into clear, actionable insights that immediately improve patient care, reimbursement and compliance. Founded only a few months ago and backed by a family office with deep healthcare expertise, we are already deployed in 180 facilities across 10+ states with paying customers.

We're building with experts from the industry who have joined the team. Our understanding of our users’ operations enables us to create products that become essential to our users' daily workflows in an industry where technological innovation has historically lagged.

At Anthuria, we foster a culture of collaboration, support, and curiosity. We assume best intentions, pursue technical excellence, and remain focused on delivering client value. As an early team member, you'll continuously shape not just our technical direction but our culture and practices as well. This is a rare opportunity to make a massive impact from the ground floor as we build something transformative in healthcare.



You Will

Build agent-powered features: Collaborate cross-functionally to build AI-powered features to enhance post-acute care operations and improve patient care. You write production-level Python code and have a strong understanding of both software engineering and machine learning principles, with a proven ability to balance experimentation rigor and rapid production deployment.

Own end-to-end evaluation loops: Establish robust evaluation frameworks and metrics for our AI solutions, empowering the team to rigorously test, monitor, and measure the performance, iterate rapidly, and deliver high-performing AI products. You dive deep into product specs, user behavior and keep the company strategy and objectives in mind at all times.

Shape a metrics-driven culture: Help establish data pipelines, product analytics, and experimentation standards that keep the entire team focused on measurable impact. You can effectively communicate technical concepts to diverse stakeholders.

Navigate healthcare systems and data requirements: Embed security and HIPAA-grade compliance into every AI system, pipeline and experiment, ensuring patient information is protected as we scale.

Drive continuous AI innovation: Participate in ongoing research into customer workflows and proactively identify emerging AI technologies that can drive business value for Anthuria.


You Are

Highly accountable: You take ownership of your work from concept to maintenance, treating the company's success as your own. You approach challenges with an owner's mindset, taking responsibility for outcomes and maintaining the highest standards of integrity in all you do.

Curious and collaborative: You continuously learn while generously offering help without being asked. You bring a growth mindset to everything you do, passionate about developing your own capabilities while lifting the entire organization in the process.

Comfortable with ambiguity: You navigate uncertainty with confidence and excitement, seeing the opportunity to shape a growing company's foundation. You are independent and self-directed, thriving in an early-stage environment where your impact is immediately visible.

Value-driven: You ship quickly without sacrificing necessary quality. You pragmatically balance speed of iteration with high technical standards, always keeping business impact as your north star when making decisions.



What We Offer

Pay Transparency

The annual base salary range for this position is $190,000-$255,000. Actual compensation offered to the successful candidate may vary from the posted hiring range based upon work experience, and/or skill level, among other things.

Benefits

 - $0 deductible and 100% employee covered health, vision, and dental benefit package

 - 401(k)

notes: TriEdge Investments is a family office and technology company with a mix of tech and non-tech roles, including engineering, sales, and healthcare positions.
